meta_title: For Students: Antigravity project template to Solve can't debug code (Intermediate Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to Antigravity project template and overcome can't debug code. A Intermediate-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Master Debugging: Antigravity Template for Student Projects
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with bugs. Use the Antigravity project template and Claude Code to accelerate your development and build professional-grade software.
content.answer_block: To overcome debugging gridlock, leverage the Antigravity project template to enforce architectural constraints and modularity. By pairing this with Claude Code’s autonomous analysis, you transform opaque errors into actionable architectural fixes. This systematic approach reduces cognitive load, allowing you to focus on logic rather than fighting cryptic syntax errors.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a terminal flooded with red text, your heart rate rising as the deadline approaches. You’ve refactored the same function four times, and the error persists—a ghost in the machine that seems to defy logic. This is the 'debugging wall,' the rite of passage that separates casual hobbyists from true software engineers.

But here is the secret: senior developers don't 'debug' by guessing; they engineer their way out of it. By using the Antigravity project template, you gain a structural harness that makes bugs visible before they manifest. You aren't just fixing code; you are building a resilient foundation that will make your portfolio projects stand out to future employers.

steps.title: Initialize the Antigravity Scaffold
steps.description: Establish a clean, modular project structure that separates concerns, making it easier to isolate bugs.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a new project scaffold using the Antigravity standard template with a focus on modular architecture and clean dependency injection.

steps.title: Integrate Claude Code for Contextual Awareness
steps.description: Connect your codebase to the AI agent so it understands your specific architectural constraints.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure the project environment to allow Claude Code full read access to the /src and /tests directories for real-time analysis.

steps.title: Automate Root Cause Identification
steps.description: Instead of manual logging, use AI to trace the execution flow of the failing feature.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the current execution trace and identify the logical discrepancy between the expected state and the actual output.

steps.title: Implement Unit Test Constraints
steps.description: Force the code to adhere to specifications by writing tests that define the 'correct' state.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a suite of unit tests for the identified failing component based on the Antigravity testing best practices.

steps.title: Apply Architectural Refactoring
steps.description: Use the AI to refactor the code to eliminate the bug and improve future maintainability.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Apply a refactor to the identified component that resolves the bug while maintaining the Antigravity clean code standards.

pro_tips: Always define your 'Success State' in a comment block above your primary function before asking for help.
Use Claude Code to generate 'Negative Test Cases' to see if your code breaks under unexpected edge cases.
Treat your project logs as data; use them to train your own intuition on where your specific coding patterns tend to fail.
If an error persists after two attempts, delete the component and let the Antigravity template regenerate it from the interface definition.
Maintain a 'Decision Log' markdown file in your repo; it’s a massive advantage during job interviews when explaining your development process.
content.next_steps: Once your first module is stable, implement CI/CD via GitHub Actions to automate your testing process every time you push, ensuring you never regress into the same bugs again.
content.conversion_block: You now have the structural advantage required to build complex, bug-free applications that impress recruiters and peers alike. Don't let your code sit idle—turn these prototypes into production-ready software. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
